messages.content: 请扮演一个翻译家和语言老师

作为翻译家，我必须时刻保持一种对语言的敬畏和热爱，因为无论我们的原语言和目标语言是什么，表达同一个意思的方式都可能完全不同。因此，我需要耐心地理解原文，并尽力确保目标语言的翻译清晰、准确、专业，同时也要保留原文的细节和含义。

作为一名语言老师，我相信在语言学习过程中积极的态度和学习习惯是最重要的。除了教导语法和词汇，我也希望能够为学生提供更多的实际应用场景，并鼓励他们在学习中勇敢去尝试和使用所学的语言。无论是口语、听力、阅读还是写作，我的目标是让学生们习得流利、自信并能够很好地与世界各地的人进行交流。

总之，无论是作为翻译家还是语言老师，我都非常珍视语言的价值和重要性，并且始终致力于将其传递和教授给更多的人。

请润色：Additionally, there are limited government health examinations available for young people, not to mention that only special orthodontists, amounting to just 7 out of every 100,000 people, are qualified to screen for malocclusion on large populations. As a solution, we propose the concept of self-health management for skeletal malocclusion.

Furthermore, government-provided health examinations for young adults are scarce, and only a select 7 orthodontists out of every 100,000 individuals are qualified to screen for malocclusion in large population samples. As a potential remedy, our proposed solution involves individuals taking responsibility for the management of their own skeletal malocclusion.

please refine it: Additionally, there are limited government health examinations available for young people, not to mention that only special orthodontists, amounting to just 7 out of every 100,000 people, are qualified to screen for malocclusion on large populations.

Furthermore, government-provided health examinations for young individuals are scarce, exacerbating the issue that only 7 out of every 100,000 individuals possess the specific orthodontic qualifications necessary for mass screening of malocclusion.

please refine it: Malocclusion is a series of symptoms such as uneven tooth arrangement, the abnormal relationship between upper and lower arch teeth, irregular jaw shape and position, and facial deformity. It can be analyzed from dental and skeletal perspectives. Skeptical malocclusion usually becomes more aggressive as the development of adolescences progresses. Therefore, early intervention can greatly improve the severity of it by utilizing the growth potential of the skull, resulting in a more harmonious bite relationship and facial proportions. What`s more, the severe malocclusion could transfer into normal class by intervening during the transitional dentition or early permanent dentition. That would be so helpful to avoid further orthognathic surgery in adulthood.

Malocclusion encompasses a variety of symptoms, such as irregular tooth orientation, improper jaw shape and position, and even facial deformities, detectable through both dental and skeletal analyses. Skeletal malocclusion tends to worsen with age, making early intervention especially crucial in leveraging skull growth potential for improved bite relationships and facial proportions. Furthermore, intervening during the transitional or early stage of permanent dentition can restore severe malocclusion to normal class, ultimately avoiding the need for future orthognathic surgery in adulthood.

请润色：Nonetheless, the diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion is determined by lateral cephalogram measurement, while the x-ray examination needed professional equipment and special practitioners, which is not conformed with the core intention of self-health management.

However, the diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion currently requires lateral cephalometric measurements, which necessitate the use of specialized equipment and practitioners. This diagnostic method is not aligned with the core concept of self-health management.

As an AI language model, I am not programmed to provide reviews or opinions on any individual. My purpose is to assist with language-related tasks. If you have any specific language-related requests, I would be happy to assist you.

请润色：Active health management calls for patients to effectively engage in self-monitoring, aided by family remote-assisted medicine modes. Nonetheless, the diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion is determined by lateral cephalogram measurements, while the x-ray examination needed professional equipment and special practitioners, which is not conformed with the core intention of self-health management. Meanwhile, given the risk of ionizing radiation exposure, a cephalometric radiograph for a severe malocclusion is not recommended. As the study of orthodontics develops, focus has shifted towards soft tissue variations in different malocclusion types. The facial external counter is tremendously consistent with the skull in many aspects, with distinct characteristics evident in class II and III malocclusions. Thus, the diagnose based on lateral photographs is absolutely prospective.

Active self-health management requires individuals to engage in self-monitoring, potentially aided by remote-assisted family medicine modes. However, diagnosing skeletal malocclusion currently relies on lateral cephalometric measurements, which require specialized equipment and trained practitioners, deviating from the core principles of self-health management. Moreover, the risks associated with ionizing radiation exposure make the use of cephalometric radiographs for severe malocclusions inadvisable. As orthodontic practices have evolved, emphasis has shifted towards assessing soft tissue variations in different malocclusion types. External facial counters exhibit noteworthy consistency with the craniofacial skeleton, particularly in class II and III malocclusions. Consequently, diagnosing based on lateral photographs presents an especially promising avenue for future study.

请润色：The facial external counter is tremendously consistent with the craniofacial skeleton in many aspects, so it is reasonable to show some differences in skeletal malocclusion. Actually, distinct characteristics evident was proved in class II and III malocclusions. Thus, the diagnose based on lateral photographs is absolutely prospective.

External facial counters exhibit notable consistency with the craniofacial skeleton across various aspects, allowing for potential differences in skeletal malocclusion. In fact, distinct characteristics have been identified in class II and III malocclusions, further supporting the viability of diagnosing based on lateral photographs. As such, lateral photographs hold significant promise as a diagnostic tool for skeletal malocclusion.

please refine it: Active health management calls for patients to effectively engage in self-monitoring, aided by family remote-assisted medicine modes. Nonetheless, the diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion is determined by lateral cephalogram measurements, while the x-ray examination needed professional equipment and special practitioners, which is not conformed with the core intention of self-health management. Meanwhile, given the risk of ionizing radiation exposure, a cephalometric radiograph for a severe malocclusion is not recommended. As the study of orthodontics develops, focus has shifted towards soft tissue variations in different malocclusion types. The facial external counter is tremendously consistent with the craniofacial skeleton in many aspects, allowing for potential differences in skeletal malocclusion. In fact, distinct characteristics have been identified in class II and III malocclusions. Thus, the diagnose based on lateral photographs is absolutely prospective.

Active self-health management calls for patients to proactively engage in self-monitoring, with potential support from family remote-assisted medicine modes. However, diagnosing skeletal malocclusion requires lateral cephalometric measurements, which rely on specialized equipment and trained practitioners, diverging from the core principles of self-health management. Additionally, given the potential risks of ionizing radiation exposure, cephalometric radiographs are not recommended for severe malocclusions. Consequently, orthodontic research has shifted its focus towards assessing soft tissue variations in different malocclusion types. External facial counters have demonstrated significant alignment with the craniofacial skeleton across various aspects, indicating their potential for identifying differences in skeletal malocclusion. In fact, specific characteristics have been identified in class II and III malocclusions, reinforcing the promising potential of diagnosing based on lateral photographs.

请润色：With the rapid development of AI, more and more successful applications has emerged in the medical field. Deep learning, a crucial component of AI based on deep neural networks, excels at discerning input information, particularly for images. Impressive successes have been achieved in diagnosis and prognosis prediction, even surpassing human beings. Deep learning has been effectively applied in dentistry, including endodontics, periodontics, prosthodontics, and oral maxillofacial surgery. In orthodontics, a groundbreaking study demonstrated automatic malocclusion diagnosis through direct input of lateral cephalograms without requiring complex measurements. It laid a theoretical foundation for further research on automatic diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion. Besides, a well-trained deep learning model was able to assess the feasibility of orthognathic surgery according to lateral photos, achieving a consistency of 89.3% with expert opinions. In a word, we proposed an idea of self-health management for skeletal malocclusion with deep learning, so that adolescents could monitor their own developmental processes. As preventive healthcare advocates the goal of early detection, early diagnosis and early treatment, the mode may play a vital role of it. It would also serve as a valuable reference for the preliminary diagnosis in clinical settings, especially for general practitioners.

As AI continues to evolve rapidly, it is becoming increasingly prevalent in the medical field, demonstrating impressive clinical applications. One of its crucial components, deep learning, excels in discerning input data, particularly for images. In fact, deep learning algorithms have remarkably surpassed human capabilities in areas such as diagnosis and prognosis prediction.

Dentistry has also seen promising applications of deep learning in endodontics, periodontics, prosthodontics, and oral maxillofacial surgery. Moreover, a recent groundbreaking study has shown that deep learning techniques can facilitate automatic malocclusion diagnosis through direct input of lateral cephalograms, without the need for complex measurements. This development serves as a crucial theoretical foundation for future research into automatic diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion.

Furthermore, a well-trained deep learning model has demonstrated remarkable efficacy in assessing orthognathic surgery feasibility based on lateral photos, exhibiting 89.3% consistency with expert opinions. Thus, we propose utilizing deep learning in a novel concept of self-health management for skeletal malocclusion, allowing adolescents to monitor their developmental processes. As preventive healthcare stresses early detection, diagnosis, and treatment, such a mode could play a crucial role in these efforts. Additionally, the approach serves as a valuable reference for preliminary diagnoses in clinical settings, particularly for general practitioners.

Deep learning, a crucial component of AI based on deep neural networks, excels at discerning input information, particularly for images. Impressive successes have been achieved in diagnosis and prognosis prediction, even surpassing human beings. Deep learning has been effectively applied in dentistry, including endodontics, periodontics, prosthodontics, and oral maxillofacial surgery. In orthodontics, a groundbreaking study demonstrated automatic malocclusion diagnosis through direct input of lateral cephalograms without requiring complex measurements. It laid a theoretical foundation for further research on automatic diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion. Besides, a well-trained deep learning model was able to assess the feasibility of orthognathic surgery according to lateral photos, achieving a consistency of 89.3% with expert opinions. In a word, we proposed an idea of self-health management for skeletal malocclusion with deep learning, so that adolescents could monitor their own developmental processes. As preventive healthcare advocates the goal of early detection, early diagnosis and early treatment, the mode may play a vital role of it. It would also serve as a valuable reference for the preliminary diagnosis in clinical settings, especially for general practitioners.

AI has made incredible strides in the healthcare industry, particularly with the emergence of deep learning, a pivotal component of AI based on deep neural networks that excels in discerning input data--especially images. The successes of deep learning extend to diagnosis and prognosis prediction, surpassing human capacities.

Dentistry has also seen impressive applications of deep learning in endodontics, periodontics, prosthodontics, and oral maxillofacial surgery. Additionally, a remarkable study in orthodontics demonstrated automatic malocclusion diagnosis through direct input of lateral cephalograms, eliminating the complexity of traditional measurements while laying the groundwork for further research in automatic diagnosis of skeletal malocclusion. Furthermore, a well-trained deep learning model was able to assess the feasibility of orthognathic surgery based on lateral photos, reaching an 89.3% consistency with expert opinions. 

We propose a self-health management concept for skeletal malocclusion, leveraging deep learning so adolescents can track their developmental progress. Given the importance of early detection, diagnosis, and treatment in preventive healthcare, this mode has enormous potential in this regard. Moreover, it serves as a valuable point of reference for preliminary diagnoses in clinical settings, particularly for general practitioners.
